# UC-22.4.3 · CCPA Sensitive Personal Information Processing Audit

## Description

Surfaces DLP policy hits from Microsoft 365 to demonstrate monitoring and limitation controls around sensitive personal information processing.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for CCPA/CPRA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes o365 and sourcetypes ms:o365:management. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Enable Office 365 Management Activity inputs in TA 4055 and confirm `Workload="Dlp"` events are ingested; (2) map `SensitiveInfoType` values to your CCPA SPI categories via lookup; (3) alert on high-severity exfil patterns; (4) retain per legal hold requirements. Indexes required: `index=o365`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ms:o365:management`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=o365` `sourcetype="ms:o365:management"` (Workload, Operation, PolicyName, UserPrincipalName, SensitiveInfoType, Severity).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Enable Office 365 Management Activity inputs in TA 4055 and confirm `Workload="Dlp"` events are ingested; (2) map `SensitiveInfoType` values to your CCPA SPI categories via lookup; (3) alert on high-severity exfil patterns; (4) retain per legal hold requirements.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" Workload="Dlp"
| stats count by PolicyName, UserPrincipalName, SensitiveInfoType, Severity, Operation
| sort - count
| table PolicyName, UserPrincipalName, SensitiveInfoType, Severity, count, Operation
```

Understanding this SPL

**CCPA Sensitive Personal Information Processing Audit (§1798.121)** — Surfaces DLP policy hits from Microsoft 365 to demonstrate monitoring and limitation controls around sensitive personal information processing.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=o365` `sourcetype="ms:o365:management"` (Workload, Operation, PolicyName, UserPrincipalName, SensitiveInfoType, Severity). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: o365; **sourcetype**: ms:o365:management. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=o365, sourcetype="ms:o365:management". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **PolicyName, UserPrincipalName, SensitiveInfoType, Severity, Operation**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: Operation, PolicyName, SensitiveInfoType, Severity, UserPrincipalName, count.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the DLP or Microsoft 365 compliance center and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console for the same window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (events by PolicyName), Heatmap (user x SensitiveInfoType), Line chart (daily volume by Severity).

## SPL

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" Workload="Dlp"
| stats count by PolicyName, UserPrincipalName, SensitiveInfoType, Severity, Operation
| sort - count
| table PolicyName, UserPrincipalName, SensitiveInfoType, Severity, count, Operation
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (events by PolicyName), Heatmap (user x SensitiveInfoType), Line chart (daily volume by Severity).
